definition: NASA-developed Hall-effect ion thruster producing 12.5 kW each; four thrusters installed on PPE for NRHO orbit maintenance and transit; developed by Aerojet Rocketdyne under NASA Glenn Research Center contract
NASA program to return humans to the Moon using SLS Orion Gateway and Human Landing System; Gateway is one of three primary Artemis program elements
Cybersecurity accreditation under FISMA (Federal Information Security Modernization Act) certifying that a system is authorized for operation after security risk assessment
The approved documented cost estimate against which program cost performance is measured; established at Program Commitment Agreement (PCA) milestone M-009
Government space agency of Canada; contributing Canadarm3 robotic system to Gateway under Implementing Arrangement; crew access rights provided in exchange
Next-generation robotic arm system contributed by CSA including 17m large arm and small dexterous arm with AI autonomy; built by MDA Space
The point at which a bodys total weight is concentrated; critical for PPE attitude control authority calculations especially when Starship HLS is docked (R-002)
Spacecraft subsystem responsible for receiving commands distributing to subsystems collecting telemetry and storing data; PPE C&DH in WBS 1.3.7
Document describing the operational concept for a system including users activities workflows and environment; Gateway ConOps is CD-022
Formal review body that governs changes to the program baseline; changes above threshold require CCB approval before implementation
List of data items required to be delivered under a contract; specifies format frequency and recipient for each deliverable; governs all technical document deliveries
Government representative designated to monitor technical performance on a contract and serve as technical liaison between the government and contractor
Document defining scope assumptions boundaries and data requirements for an independent cost estimate; gateway CARD is CD-041; required per NPR 7120.5F
EVM metric equal to BCWP divided by ACWP; CPI less than 1.0 indicates cost overrun; Northrop Grumman HALO CPI below 1.0 per 2023 contractor charges (R-003)
Formal review at which design is sufficiently mature to proceed to fabrication; PPE CDR at M-010; HALO CDR at M-011; I-HAB CDR at M-016
Current mass or cost estimate reflecting latest design information before margins are applied; contrasted with Predicted Mass or cost at completion
NASA global antenna network providing communications with deep space missions; operated by JPL from Goldstone CA Madrid Spain and Canberra Australia; Gateway integration per T-142
Change in velocity; measure of propulsive effort for orbital maneuvers in meters per second; NRHO station-keeping requires ~10 m/s per year per T-038 analysis
Specific mission scenario used as basis for system design requirements; Gateway DRM encompasses NRHO operations Artemis crewed missions and visiting vehicle support
Canadarm3 small arm enabling fine manipulation servicing and inspection; AI-enabled; key component of WBS 1.8.2
Project management technique integrating scope schedule and cost using BCWS BCWP and ACWP; required for all Gateway cost-reimbursable contracts above threshold

term: Environmental Control and Life Support System
definition: System providing breathable atmosphere water recovery temperature control fire detection and waste management for crew; HALO ECLSS in 1.4.2; I-HAB ECLSS in 1.5.2
Multi-national European space organization; contributing I-HAB and ESPRIT to Gateway; formal Implementing Arrangement with NASA; managed from ESTEC Netherlands

term: European System Providing Refueling Infrastructure and Telecommunications
definition: ESA contribution consisting of Lunar Link (launched 2027 pre-attached to HALO) and Lunar View module (2029) providing communications and xenon propellant refueling
Upper stage for SLS Block 1B providing additional performance to lunar orbit; required for I-HAB co-manifested launch on Artemis IV; development risk identified as R-013
Spacewalk performed by crew outside pressurized spacecraft; enabled from Gateway via UAE Airlock (1.7); Canadarm3 (1.8) provides crew EVA support and inspection
Systematic analysis identifying potential failure modes their causes and effects on system performance; PPE FMEA is CD-017; HALO FMEA is CD-018
SpaceX heavy-lift launch vehicle using three Falcon 9 first stage cores; used for PPE-HALO launch; $331.8M contract; expendable Block 5 configuration for Gateway mission
Onboard software detecting hardware failures isolating the fault and initiating autonomous recovery; critical for 200-day uncrewed Gateway operations between crewed missions
US law requiring federal agencies to develop implement and maintain information security programs; Gateway MCC-H requires FISMA-compliant accreditation (ATO) per T-145
US government fiscal year running October 1 through September 30; all Gateway cost phasing and budget requests organized by FY; program spans FY2019-FY2033
Facility at GSFC providing NRHO trajectory determination stationkeeping maneuver planning orbit maintenance and conjunction assessment; critical for T-134 and T-135
Flight directors flight controllers and supporting specialists providing 24/7 coverage during crewed Gateway missions; certification completed under T-150
Independent congressional watchdog; issued GAO-24-106878 (July 2024) identifying PPE-HALO mass risk (R-001) and schedule concerns; drove T-009 and T-015
NASA program office at Johnson Space Center responsible for managing all elements of the Lunar Gateway program; led by program manager reporting to ESD AA
Test and handling equipment used at manufacturing facilities and launch sites; not flight hardware but critical for processing; WBS 1.9.6
First crewed pressurized module; built by Northrop Grumman (structure by TAS Turin); $1.122B total; 5 IDS docking ports; launched 2027 co-stacked with PPE
Type of ion thruster using crossed electric and magnetic fields to ionize and accelerate xenon propellant; high specific impulse ~3000s; used in PPE AEPS system
Lunar lander vehicle; SpaceX Starship selected as NASA HLS; docks at Gateway for crew transfer to lunar surface; 18x mass ratio to PPE design value identified in R-002
Process of certifying a spacecraft or system as safe for human occupancy; Gateway elements must comply with NPR 8705.2C and NASA-STD-3001; drives T-022 and T-023
Bilateral agreement between NASA and a partner space agency defining contributions responsibilities and crew access rights for Gateway; signed with ESA JAXA CSA MBRSC
Cost estimate prepared by independent organization not the program office or prime contractor; Gateway ICE performed by Aerospace Corporation (CD-042) prior to PCA (M-009)
Formal review verifying that contractor performance measurement baseline is realistic traceable and executable; PPE IBR at T-005; HALO IBR at T-006
Comprehensive networked schedule integrating all program activities from all contracts and organizations; 165+ tasks; maintained in Primavera P6; document CD-025
Technical document defining interface between two systems; governs mechanical electrical thermal and data interfaces; Gateway IRD and all element ICDs tracked in CD-004 through CD-010
Common docking interface standard derived from IDSS allowing different nations vehicles to dock with each other; all Gateway ports use IDS; drives T-056 and T-066
Japanese space agency contributing ECLSS hardware for I-HAB (WBS 1.5.2) valued at approximately ¥15B; also contributing JAXA rover for lunar surface exploration
NASA center in Houston Texas; home of Gateway Program Office (GPO); primary human spaceflight mission control center (MCC-H) for crewed operations
NASA launch facility in Florida; launch site for PPE-HALO on Falcon Heavy (T-126 through T-132); payload processing per T-127 at PHSF
Final major review before launch certifying vehicle spacecraft ground systems and operations team are ready; PPE-HALO LRR at M-023 per T-131
Canadian aerospace company (formerly MacDonald Dettwiler and Associates); building Canadarm3 under CSA contract; heritage from ISS Canadarm2 and SSRMS
NASA Johnson Space Center facility housing flight control rooms; dedicated Gateway flight control room being developed under T-141; hosts ~50 controller workstations
NASA JSC directorate responsible for flight operations for all human spaceflight missions; responsible for Gateway WBS 1.12 staffing and training
UAE government space agency contributing Gateway Lunar Airlock (WBS 1.7); announced January 2024; first UAE human spaceflight hardware contribution
Thermal insulation blanket consisting of multiple thin layers of reflective material; used extensively on PPE (1.3.6) and all Gateway modules for cis-lunar thermal protection
Highly elliptical lunar orbit; perilune ~3366 km altitude; apolune ~71100 km; period 6.56 days; near-stable requiring only ~10 m/s per year stationkeeping
Independent technical and cost review performed before a Program Commitment Agreement; Gateway NAR performed by Aerospace Corporation (T-008) completing at M-008
Division of Northrop Grumman Corporation serving as HALO prime contractor; integration facility at Gilbert Arizona used for PPE-HALO integration T-118 through T-125
NASA crewed spacecraft for Artemis missions built by Lockheed Martin; docks at Gateway HALO port for crew transfer; first crewed docking at M-030 on Artemis IV
KSC facility for propellant loading and hazardous operations on spacecraft; PPE-HALO processed at PHSF per T-127 and T-128 before Falcon Heavy launch
Formal review establishing design is mature enough to proceed to detailed design; PPE PDR at T-028; HALO PDR at T-048; I-HAB PDR at M-012
Solar electric propulsion bus providing primary Gateway power (~60 kW BOL) thrust and attitude control; built by Maxar Technologies under $375M FFP contract
Quantitative risk analysis using fault trees and event trees to estimate probability and consequence of undesired outcomes; drives WBS 1.2.5 reliability work
NASA internal document establishing approved scope cost and schedule baseline for a program; Gateway PCA baseline at M-009; approved by NASA ESD Associate Administrator
Document mapping system requirements to test procedures and verification evidence; maintained in DOORS per CD-001; updated at each program review
Flexible solar array blanket that deploys from a spool; higher power density than rigid panels; ISS ROSA flight demonstration heritage; used in PPE (1.3.2)
EVM metric equal to BCWP divided by BCWS; SPI less than 1.0 indicates schedule slippage; monitored monthly per T-004 and CD-025
Propulsion system using solar electricity to ionize and accelerate xenon propellant via Hall-effect thrusters; high specific impulse ~3000s; PPE primary propulsion
NASA heavy-lift launch vehicle; Block 1B with Exploration Upper Stage used for Artemis IV carrying Orion crew and I-HAB co-manifested; NASA/Boeing contract
First formal program technical review verifying system-level requirements are complete consistent and testable; Gateway SRR at M-002 per T-011
NASA 9-level scale for technology maturity; TRL 6 required at CDR; TRL 9 proven in operational environment; Canadarm3 AI autonomy (R-011) must reach TRL 6 at CDR
Prime contractor for HALO structure I-HAB ESPRIT and UAE Airlock; Italian-French joint venture between Thales and Leonardo; extensive ISS module heritage
Propulsive maneuver accelerating spacecraft from Earth parking orbit to lunar transfer trajectory; PPE-HALO placed on NRHO transit trajectory after T-132 Falcon Heavy launch
Program-level management reserve not allocated to any WBS; Gateway UFE is ~$1755M (21% of base estimate); released through formal program change process
Document defining how all system requirements will be verified via test analysis inspection or demonstration; Gateway V&V Plan is CD-021 updated version
Noble gas used as propellant in Hall-effect and ion thrusters; PPE carries xenon for 15-year NRHO life; ESPRIT Lunar View (1.6.2) stores xenon for PPE refueling
